vocabulary.example_sentence: Unsupervised learning can group customers by their shopping behaviour.

vocabulary.definition_ar: يرقي، يحسن أو يستبدل عتاد أو برمجيات الكمبيوتر بنسخة أفضل
vocabulary.example_sentence: The company decided to upgrade their old computers to newer models.

vocabulary.definition_ar: افتراضي، موجود في أجهزة الكمبيوتر أو على الإنترنت وليس في العالم الفيزيائي
vocabulary.example_sentence: Students attended a virtual conference from their homes.

vocabulary.definition_ar: تكيفي، قادر على التغيير أو التعديل استجابة لظروف أو أحوال مختلفة
vocabulary.example_sentence: Adaptive AI systems can learn and improve their performance over time.

vocabulary.definition_ar: مستقل، قادر على العمل بشكل مستقل دون تحكم أو تدخل بشري
vocabulary.example_sentence: Autonomous vehicles can drive themselves using sensors and AI technology.

vocabulary.definition_ar: معيار مرجعي، نقطة مرجعية أو معيار يُستخدم لقياس ومقارنة جودة أو أداء شيء ما
vocabulary.example_sentence: Researchers use specific tests as benchmarks to evaluate how well AI systems perform.

vocabulary.definition_ar: تحيز، تفضيل غير عادل لصالح أو ضد شيء ما، غالباً بناءً على معلومات محدودة
vocabulary.example_sentence: AI systems can show bias if they are trained on incomplete data.

vocabulary.definition_ar: روبوت محادثة، برنامج حاسوبي مصمم للحديث مع البشر عبر النص أو الصوت
vocabulary.example_sentence: The company's chatbot answers customer questions 24 hours a day.

vocabulary.definition_ar: التصنيف، عملية وضع الأشياء في فئات أو مجموعات بناءً على خصائصها
vocabulary.example_sentence: Image classification allows AI to identify different objects in photographs automatically.

vocabulary.definition_ar: معرفي، متعلق بالعمليات الذهنية للتفكير والفهم والتعلم والتذكر
vocabulary.example_sentence: AI systems try to copy human cognitive abilities like reasoning and learning.

vocabulary.definition_ar: يصادف، يجد أو يواجه شيئاً بالصدفة أو بشكل غير متوقع
vocabulary.example_sentence: While researching AI, scientists often come across unexpected discoveries about intelligence.

vocabulary.definition_ar: حاسوبي، متعلق بالحسابات الرياضية أو يتضمنها، خاصة بواسطة الحاسوب
vocabulary.example_sentence: Computational models help scientists understand how the human brain processes information.

vocabulary.definition_ar: النقطة التي يتوقف فيها نموذج التعلم الآلي عن التحسن بشكل ملحوظ أثناء التدريب.
vocabulary.example_sentence: Engineers watch for convergence to know when training is complete.

vocabulary.definition_ar: علم التحكم الآلي، علم التواصل والتحكم في الآلات والكائنات الحية
vocabulary.example_sentence: Cybernetics combines computer science with biology to create smarter machines.

vocabulary.definition_ar: تقنية تُنشئ تنوعات في بيانات التدريب الحالية لتحسين نموذج الذكاء الاصطناعي.
vocabulary.example_sentence: Data augmentation can flip or rotate images to create more training examples.

vocabulary.definition_ar: التعلم العميق، نوع من التعلم الآلي يستخدم الشبكات العصبية الاصطناعية للتعلم من البيانات
vocabulary.example_sentence: Deep learning helps computers understand speech and images better than before.

vocabulary.definition_ar: تمثيل رقمي للكلمات أو المفاهيم يلتقط معناها للذكاء الاصطناعي.
vocabulary.example_sentence: Word embeddings allow AI to understand that 'king' and 'queen' are related.

vocabulary.definition_ar: تمريرة كاملة واحدة عبر مجموعة بيانات التدريب بأكملها أثناء التعلم الآلي.
vocabulary.example_sentence: The model needed fifty epochs before achieving good accuracy.

vocabulary.definition_ar: أخلاقي، متعلق بالمبادئ الأخلاقية حول ما هو صحيح وخطأ
vocabulary.example_sentence: Scientists discuss ethical concerns about using AI in medical decisions.

vocabulary.definition_ar: الدرجة التي يمكن بها فهم الآلية الداخلية لنموذج الذكاء الاصطناعي من قِبَل البشر.
vocabulary.example_sentence: Explainability is crucial in medical AI so doctors can trust the output.

vocabulary.definition_ar: التعرف على الوجوه، تقنية يمكنها تحديد هوية الأشخاص بتحليل ملامح وجوههم
vocabulary.example_sentence: Many phones now use facial recognition to unlock the screen securely.

vocabulary.definition_ar: أسلوب تحسين يُستخدم لتقليل الأخطاء في نموذج التعلم الآلي أثناء التدريب.
vocabulary.example_sentence: Gradient descent adjusts the model's weights to reduce its mistakes.

vocabulary.definition_ar: إعداد يُختار قبل التدريب يتحكم في طريقة تعلم نموذج التعلم الآلي.
vocabulary.example_sentence: Choosing the right hyperparameter can significantly improve model results.

vocabulary.definition_ar: الاستنتاج، خلاصة أو رأي متكون بناءً على الأدلة والمنطق وليس المشاهدة المباشرة
vocabulary.example_sentence: AI systems use inference to make logical conclusions from the data they analyze.

vocabulary.definition_ar: الجزء من نظام الذكاء الاصطناعي الذي يطبق القواعد المكتسبة على بيانات جديدة لاتخاذ القرارات.
vocabulary.example_sentence: The inference engine processes each customer request in milliseconds.

vocabulary.definition_ar: يدمج أجزاء أو أنظمة مختلفة معاً لتعمل كوحدة موحدة
vocabulary.example_sentence: Many companies are trying to integrate AI technology into their daily operations.

vocabulary.definition_ar: التفسير، عمل شرح أو فهم معنى شيء ما
vocabulary.example_sentence: AI's interpretation of medical images can help doctors detect diseases earlier.

vocabulary.definition_ar: بديهي، سهل الفهم أو الاستخدام دون الحاجة إلى شرح أو تدريب
vocabulary.example_sentence: The AI assistant has an intuitive interface that anyone can learn quickly.

vocabulary.definition_ar: شبكة من الحقائق المترابطة يستخدمها الذكاء الاصطناعي لفهم العلاقات بين المفاهيم.
vocabulary.example_sentence: A knowledge graph links 'Paris' to 'France' and 'Eiffel Tower'.

vocabulary.definition_ar: نظام ذكاء اصطناعي يستطيع معالجة أنواع مختلفة من البيانات ودمجها مثل النصوص والصور.
vocabulary.example_sentence: Multimodal AI can describe a photo and answer questions about it.

vocabulary.definition_ar: فرع من فروع الذكاء الاصطناعي يُمكِّن الحواسيب من فهم اللغة البشرية والاستجابة لها.
vocabulary.example_sentence: Natural language processing powers voice assistants and translation apps.

vocabulary.definition_ar: المسار العصبي، سلسلة من الخلايا العصبية المترابطة التي تنقل المعلومات في الدماغ أو الجهاز العصبي
vocabulary.example_sentence: AI researchers study neural pathways to understand how human thinking works.

vocabulary.definition_ar: يحسن، يجعل شيئاً جيداً أو فعالاً أو وظيفياً قدر الإمكان
vocabulary.example_sentence: The AI system can optimize energy usage in smart buildings automatically.

vocabulary.definition_ar: التخصيص، عملية جعل شيء مناسباً لحاجات أو تفضيلات شخص معين
vocabulary.example_sentence: AI enables better personalization of online shopping recommendations for each customer.

vocabulary.definition_ar: تنبؤي، متعلق بالقدرة على التنبؤ بما سيحدث في المستقبل أو يملك هذه القدرة
vocabulary.example_sentence: Predictive AI models help companies plan their business strategies more effectively.

vocabulary.definition_ar: في الوقت الفعلي، يحدث فوراً دون أي تأخير بين الإدخال والإخراج
vocabulary.example_sentence: AI translation apps can provide real-time language conversion during conversations.

vocabulary.definition_ar: طريقة تعلم آلي يتعلم فيها وكيل الذكاء الاصطناعي من خلال تلقي المكافآت أو العقوبات.
vocabulary.example_sentence: Reinforcement learning taught the robot to walk without falling.

vocabulary.definition_ar: ممارسة تصميم أنظمة الذكاء الاصطناعي واستخدامها بطرق عادلة وآمنة وأخلاقية.
vocabulary.example_sentence: Responsible AI means checking systems for bias before releasing them.

vocabulary.definition_ar: قابل للتوسيع، يمكن زيادته في الحجم أو العدد أو النطاق مع الحفاظ على الفعالية
vocabulary.example_sentence: Cloud-based AI solutions are scalable and can handle growing amounts of data.

vocabulary.definition_ar: تحليل المشاعر، استخدام الذكاء الاصطناعي لفهم المشاعر أو الآراء المعبر عنها في النص
vocabulary.example_sentence: Companies use sentiment analysis to understand what customers think about their products.

vocabulary.definition_ar: يبسط، يجعل عملية أبسط أو أكثر كفاءة أو أسهل للقيام بها
vocabulary.example_sentence: AI helps streamline business operations by automating repetitive tasks.

vocabulary.definition_ar: المراقبة، المتابعة الدقيقة لشخص أو مكان أو نشاط، خاصة من قبل الشرطة أو الحكومة
vocabulary.example_sentence: AI-powered surveillance cameras can detect suspicious behavior in public areas.

vocabulary.definition_ar: يأخذ في الحسبان، يعتبر أو يدرج شيئاً عند اتخاذ قرار أو حكم
vocabulary.example_sentence: AI systems must take into account privacy concerns when processing personal data.

vocabulary.definition_ar: نوع من تصميمات الشبكات العصبية تعالج فيه جميع أجزاء التسلسل في وقت واحد.
vocabulary.example_sentence: Most modern language models rely on transformer architecture.

vocabulary.definition_ar: متعدد الاستخدامات، قادر على التكيف أو الاستخدام لأغراض أو حالات مختلفة كثيرة
vocabulary.example_sentence: Modern AI systems are versatile enough to help with writing, coding, and analysis.

paragraphs: Artificial intelligence has *emerged* as one of the most transformative technologies of our time, quietly *revolutionizing* how millions of people around the world conduct their daily activities. From the moment we wake up to our smartphone alarms to the time we fall asleep after watching AI-recommended videos, this powerful technology has become an *integral* part of modern life that most people take for granted.

The *scope* of AI applications continues to *expand* at an unprecedented rate across various sectors. In healthcare, AI systems can now *diagnose* certain medical conditions with accuracy rates exceeding 95%, which *surpasses* the performance of many human specialists in specific areas. Educational platforms *leverage* machine learning algorithms to create personalized learning experiences, adapting to each student's pace and learning style. Meanwhile, financial institutions have *implemented* AI-powered fraud detection systems that process millions of transactions daily, protecting consumers from cyber threats that would be impossible for humans to monitor manually.

What makes AI particularly *compelling* is its ability to *process* vast amounts of data and identify patterns that humans might overlook. For instance, in Saudi Arabia's NEOM project, AI technologies are being integrated into urban planning to create smart cities that can *anticipate* residents' needs and optimize resource allocation. These systems can predict traffic patterns, manage energy consumption, and even forecast maintenance requirements for infrastructure. Such applications demonstrate how AI doesn't just automate existing processes but creates entirely new possibilities for urban development.

Despite these remarkable *breakthroughs*, experts emphasize that AI works best when it *complements* human intelligence rather than replacing it entirely. Dr. Sarah Al-Rashid, a computer science professor at King Abdulaziz University, recently stated that "the most successful AI implementations are those that enhance human capabilities while preserving the creativity and ethical judgment that only humans can provide." This perspective highlights the importance of viewing AI as a collaborative tool that can help solve complex global challenges, from climate change to healthcare accessibility, when properly guided by human wisdom and values.

Dr. Sarah Al-Mansouri *glances* at her smartphone as she walks through King Faisal Specialist Hospital in Riyadh. The notification on her screen indicates that an AI system has just *flagged* a patient's X-ray for urgent review. This scene, which would have seemed like science fiction a decade ago, is now becoming routine in hospitals across Saudi Arabia and the broader Middle East region.

The Kingdom's healthcare sector is currently *undergoing* a remarkable transformation through artificial intelligence integration. According to recent statistics from the Saudi Ministry of Health, over 40% of major hospitals have *implemented* some form of AI technology by 2024. These systems can *detect* early signs of diseases like diabetes and heart conditions with 94% accuracy – often *surpassing* human doctors in speed and precision. Dr. Al-Mansouri explains that the AI assistant doesn't *replace* medical professionals but rather *enhances* their capabilities. "When I *encounter* a complex case, the AI provides additional insights that help me make more informed decisions," she reports.

The *adoption* of AI in Middle Eastern healthcare faces unique challenges that researchers are working to *address*. Cultural considerations play a significant role, as patients often prefer discussing sensitive health matters with human doctors rather than digital systems. However, younger generations appear more willing to *embrace* these technological advances. A recent survey conducted across the Gulf Cooperation Council countries revealed that 73% of respondents under 30 would trust AI-powered diagnostic tools, compared to only 45% of those over 50. Healthcare providers are *striving* to balance technological innovation with traditional patient care values.

Looking toward the future, experts predict that AI will become even more *sophisticated* in addressing region-specific health challenges. The technology is being trained to recognize diseases that are more prevalent in the Middle East, such as certain genetic conditions and diabetes complications common in the region. Dubai's Health Authority has announced plans to invest $2.3 billion in AI healthcare initiatives over the next five years, while Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030 includes ambitious goals for digital health transformation. As Dr. Al-Mansouri concludes her hospital rounds, she reflects on how these intelligent systems are not just changing medicine – they're potentially saving thousands of lives across the region.

sections.content_ar: الماضي البسيط: للأحداث المنتهية في وقت محدد في الماضي • الماضي التام: للأحداث التي حدثت في الماضي لكن لها تأثير على الحاضر • استخدم الماضي التام مع: since, for, already, yet • استخدم الماضي البسيط مع: yesterday, ago, last week
sections.content_en:
Present Perfect vs Past Simple

Examples:
• AI technology has changed our lives dramatically. (focus on the result now)
• Scientists developed the first AI program in 1956. (specific time in the past)
• ChatGPT has become very popular since its launch. (from past until now)

When to use Present Perfect:
- Actions that happened at an unspecified time in the past but are relevant now
- Actions that started in the past and continue to the present
- Recent actions with present results
- With words like: since, for, already, yet, just, ever, never

When to use Past Simple:
- Actions completed at a specific time in the past
- Sequential actions in the past
- With time expressions like: yesterday, last week, in 2020, ago, when

sections.content: Present Perfect: Subject + have/has + past participle | Past Simple: Subject + verb (past form)

items.sentence: Machine learning has revolutionized data analysis.
items.translation_ar: لقد أحدث التعلم الآلي ثورة في تحليل البيانات

items.sentence: Google launched its AI assistant in 2016.
items.translation_ar: أطلقت جوجل مساعدها الذكي في عام 2016

items.sentence: Self-driving cars have been tested for several years.
items.translation_ar: تم اختبار السيارات ذاتية القيادة لعدة سنوات

items.sentence: IBM created Deep Blue twenty years ago.
items.translation_ar: أنشأت آي بي إم ديب بلو قبل عشرين عاماً

items.wrong: I have visited the AI conference last week.
items.correct: I visited the AI conference last week.
items.explanation_ar: مع الوقت المحدد (last week) نستخدم الماضي البسيط

items.correct: AI technology has changed our lives.
items.explanation_ar: عندما نتحدث عن تأثير مستمر حتى الآن نستخدم الماضي التام

items.wrong: Did you ever use AI tools?
items.correct: Have you ever used AI tools?
items.explanation_ar: مع ever نستخدم الماضي التام وليس الماضي البسيط
